top_signals.title: Press Release: Renewable Shipping Fuel Supply at Risk as EU Proposal Threatens Key Delivery Pathway
top_signals.summary: Eurogas and 44 co-signatories are warning that proposed changes to EU sustainability certification rules could undermine liquefaction by equivalence, a key pathway used to supply bioLNG and eLNG to ships via existing LNG infrastructure. BioLNG and eLNG are among the few renewable fuels already compatible with current LNG-fuelled vessels and infrastructure, with demand rising sharply ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: OCBC lowers oil price outlook as Hormuz flows normalize
top_signals.summary: OCBC Group Research on Thursday cut its quarterly forecasts through the second quarter of 2027, citing a rebound in shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz that has revived worries about oversupply in the oil market. The bank now sees Brent averaging $75 a barrel in the third quarter of 2026, down from $85 previously, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Tanker Market: Venezuelan Oil Market Coming Into Play Once More
top_signals.summary: The Venezuelan market has re-entered the crude tanker trade with more robustness over the past few months. In its latest weekly report, shipbroker Xclusiv said that “the Venezuelan crude trade has moved from being a relatively narrow and politically constrained flow into one of the most important incremental demand drivers for the crude tanker market ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Swire Shipping Opens First Branch Office in Timor-Leste
top_signals.summary: Swire Shipping today announced the establishment of a new branch office in Dili, Timor-Leste, marking a significant milestone in the company’s continued investment in Southeast Asia and reinforcing its long-term commitment to one of the region’s emerging markets. The new office will be led by Darry Tan, who has spent the past two years on ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Mental Health Support Solutions highlights lack of industry-wide standards for psychological support at sea as it concludes maritime Masterclass for psychologists
top_signals.summary: Mental Health Support Solutions (MHSS), member of OneCare Group, has highlighted the absence of industry-wide standards for psychological support in shipping following the completion of a specialist online maritime psychology masterclass designed to equip mental health professionals with the knowledge needed to support seafarers. While international regulations and guidance have significantly improved physical health, safety ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Iron Ore Surplus Builds as Chinese Steel Margins Remain Under Pressure
top_signals.summary: China’s steel market continues to show signs of softness. The National Bureau of Statistics reported crude steel production fell 2.5% year-on-year in May 2026 to 84.4 million tons, while pig iron — produced via blast furnace — declined 1.5% to 72.9 million tons. Domestic conditions remain under pressure. China’s theoretical rebar margin* stays near decade ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: National Payments Vision
top_signals.summary: The government has published the National Payments Vision, setting out its ambitions for the UK’s payments sector to deliver world-leading payments and support the growth mission.

top_signals.title: BIMCO: Coal shipments jump 14% in June, driven by Chinese demand
top_signals.summary: “In June 2026, global coal shipments jumped 14% y/y, driven by a 41% y/y increase in coal shipments to China, as the country looked to offset weaker domestic supply and to meet higher demand from electricity generation. Since the start of the year, global coal shipments have risen 3% y/y, a reversal of last year’s ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: PJM on Maximum Alert to Prevent Outages Amid Extreme Heat Wave
top_signals.summary: PJM Interconnection, the operator of the largest U.S. electric grid, has issued a maximum generation alert for July 3 asking power producers to maximize electricity generation amid soaring demand in the current extreme heat wave. PJM had a Maximum Generation Alert and Load Management Alert in effect for July 2 across its footprint in the eastern U.S. and Midwest. These alerts have now been issued for the PJM footprint for July 3, too. While the alert does not require any action from customers, generators and transmission owners are asked to maximize…

top_signals.title: Panama Returns To The Paris MOU White List Through Stronger Maritime Governance And A Comprehensive Compliance Strategy
top_signals.summary: Panama has been reinstated on the White List of the Paris Memorandum of Understanding (Paris MoU), according to the organization’s recently published Annual Report. The Panama Maritime Authority (PMA) welcomed the announcement, noting that the White List is widely regarded as one of the foremost international benchmarks for measuring the performance and quality of maritime ...
